name: BLSA
full_name: Baltimore Longitudinal Study of Aging
notes: Oldest US longitudinal aging study. NIA-funded. Includes amyloid PET.

name: Knight ADRC
full_name: Knight Alzheimer Disease Research Center at WUSTL
notes: Deep phenotyping. Rich proteomics (CSF + plasma). Well-curated post-mortem.

name: WHICAP
full_name: Washington Heights–Inwood Columbia Aging Project
notes: Notably diverse cohort: Caribbean Hispanic and African American populations. NYC-based.

name: ROSMAP
full_name: Religious Orders Study and Memory and Aging Project
notes: Rich post-mortem multi-omics. Unique longitudinal clinical-to-neuropath design.

name: BIOCARD
full_name: Biomarkers of Cognitive Decline Among Normal Individuals
notes: Longitudinal study of cognitively normal volunteers at NIH. Rich longitudinal CSF biomarker data spanning 25+ years. Unique long-term follow-up.

name: NACC
full_name: National Alzheimer's Coordinating Center
notes: Harmonised data across 34 US Alzheimer's Disease Research Centers. Includes post-mortem neuropathology.

name: WRAP
full_name: Wisconsin Registry for Alzheimer's Prevention
notes: Prevention-focused registry. Parental history of AD enrichment. Longitudinal.

name: ADNI
full_name: Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative
notes: Gold-standard AD neuroimaging cohort. Multiple phases (ADNI-1, GO, 2, 3, 4). Predominantly white/European-American.

name: AddNeuroMed
full_name: AddNeuroMed
notes: Pan-European multi-site study. Good geographic diversity within Europe.

name: AIBL
full_name: Australian Imaging, Biomarker & Lifestyle Flagship Study
notes: Major Australian cohort. Strong amyloid PET and lifestyle data.

name: UK Biobank
full_name: UK Biobank
notes: Population-level biobank. Application-based access. Largest MRI+genetics cohort.

name: OASIS-1
full_name: Open Access Series of Imaging Studies — Cross-sectional
notes: Fully open access. Cross-sectional. Age range 18–96.

name: J-ADNI
full_name: Japanese Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative
notes: Japanese harmonised replication of the ADNI protocol. Enables direct comparison of biomarker profiles between Japanese and North American populations.

name: MAPT
full_name: Multidomain Alzheimer Preventive Trial
notes: French multi-domain lifestyle intervention trial in elderly at-risk individuals. Strong data on omega-3 supplementation and cognitive training. 13 sites across France.

name: SAGES
full_name: Successful AGing after Elective Surgery — Cognitive Substudy
notes: Longitudinal study examining perioperative cognitive decline. Includes diverse urban Boston population. Notably higher minority representation than most AD cohorts.

name: PREVENT-AD
full_name: Pre-symptomatic Evaluation of Experimental or Novel Treatments for Alzheimer Disease
notes: Focus on individuals at familial risk for AD. Canadian French cohort.

name: ADSP
full_name: Alzheimer's Disease Sequencing Project
notes: NIH initiative sequencing whole genomes and exomes of AD cases and controls to identify disease-causing and protective variants. Prioritises diverse populations including African American, Hispanic/Latino, and Asian individuals. Data via NIAGADS.

name: ALFA
full_name: Alzheimer and Families (ALFA) Study
notes: Catalan cohort of cognitively unimpaired adults, mostly offspring of AD patients. Led by the Barcelona Beta Brain Research Center (BBRC). Strong amyloid/tau PET and CSF biomarker data. ALFA+ sub-study with richer biomarker profiling.

name: A4 Study
full_name: Anti-Amyloid Treatment in Asymptomatic Alzheimer's
notes: Prevention trial enriched for amyloid positivity. Diverse international sites.

name: KBASE
full_name: Korean Brain Aging Study for Early Diagnosis and Prediction of Alzheimer's Disease
notes: First large-scale Korean cohort for AD biomarker research. Critical Asian-population resource for amyloid PET and genetic studies.

name: EPAD
full_name: European Prevention of Alzheimer's Dementia
notes: Prevention-focused. Longitudinal. Multi-modal imaging. European-only.

name: DELCODE
full_name: DZNE-Longitudinal Cognitive Impairment and Dementia Study
notes: German prevention study. Subjective cognitive decline enriched. 10 DZNE sites.

name: Mayo Clinic AD Study
full_name: Mayo Clinic Alzheimer's Disease Genetics Study (AMP-AD)
notes: Part of the AMP-AD consortium. RNA-seq from temporal cortex and cerebellum across AD and control brains. Deposited in Synapse. Complementary to ROSMAP for cross-cohort transcriptomic replication.

name: MSBB
full_name: Mount Sinai Brain Bank (AMP-AD)
notes: Multi-region RNA-seq and proteomics from Mount Sinai Brain Bank AD cases and controls. Part of AMP-AD. Includes 4 brain regions per donor, enabling network-based analyses. Available via Synapse.

name: LASI-DAD
full_name: Longitudinal Aging Study in India — Diagnostic Assessment of Dementia
notes: Largest population-based dementia study in India. Critical for LMIC representation.

name: H3Africa Brain
full_name: Human Heredity and Health in Africa — Brain Health
notes: Critical resource for African-descent populations. Addresses the severe gap in sub-Saharan African representation in dementia genetics. Includes genetic data from populations not represented in IGAP or EADB.

name: OASIS-3
full_name: Open Access Series of Imaging Studies — Longitudinal Neuroimaging, Clinical, and Cognitive Dataset for Normal Aging and Alzheimer Disease
notes: Longitudinal. Open access. Includes amyloid/tau PET.

name: ReDLat
full_name: Red Latina de Demencias Tempranas (Latin American Network for Early-Onset Dementia)
notes: Crucial for Latin American diversity. Includes Indigenous-descent and mestizo populations.

name: ANMerge
full_name: AddNeuroMed Merged Dataset
notes: Fully open harmonised version of AddNeuroMed. Available via Synapse.

name: SEA-AD
full_name: Seattle Alzheimer's Disease Brain Cell Atlas
notes: Single-cell and single-nucleus RNA-seq + ATAC-seq from 84 human brain donors spanning the full AD neuropathological spectrum. Produced by the Allen Institute for Brain Science. Fully open via the Allen Brain Map portal and Synapse.

name: BrainLat
full_name: Latin American Brain Health Institute Neuroimaging Dataset
notes: First open multimodal neuroimaging dataset of neurodegeneration from Latin America. Includes AD, bvFTD, PD, MS and healthy controls. MRI (anatomical, functional, diffusion) + high-density EEG. BIDS format. Hosted on Synapse. Critical resource for underrepresented population neuroscience.
